title: [InstCombine] Scalarize `(vec_ops (insert ?, X, Idx))` when only one element is demanded
summary: The developers generalized a single-case optimization into a reusable transformation by extracting a core pattern: replacing a shuffle that blends a scalarized operation into a vector with direct insertelement instructions. They implemented `getScalarizationOfInsertElement` to recursively traverse through unary ops, casts, binops, and cmps, scalarizing operations when safe (single-use, speculatable). This allows the optimization to handle diverse operations (fdiv, frem, udiv, casts) and complex chains while preserving flags and avoiding duplication. The key insight was separating pattern matching from scalarization logic, enabling the same infrastructure to work across multiple instruction types and operand configurations.
